<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#cfefff"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#b7e2ff"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#7fc6a8"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="ground" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#2f7d4a"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#1f4f35"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-20%" y="-20%" width="140%" height="140%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="3"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#sky)"/>

  <!-- distant haze / treeline -->
  <path d="M0,285 C60,260 120,270 180,255 C240,240 300,255 360,250 C430,243 470,260 512,248 L512,512 L0,512 Z" fill="#2a6b43" opacity="0.35" filter="url(#soft)"/>

  <!-- ground base -->
  <path d="M0,330 C80,315 160,340 240,330 C320,320 400,350 512,330 L512,512 L0,512 Z" fill="url(#ground)"/>

  <!-- trees back layer -->
  <g opacity="0.95">
    <g transform="translate(40,160)">
      <rect x="18" y="150" width="14" height="90" rx="5" fill="#1b3b2a"/>
      <polygon points="25,150 5,195 45,195" fill="#2c7a4e"/>

    <g transform="translate(250,170)">

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG forest design**

I need to make an SVG drawing that represents a forest without any text. I’m thinking of using tree silhouettes, layered hills, and some foliage. It's important that there are absolutely no letters. I want to incorporate shapes like circles, triangles, and paths. The SVG should be 512x512. I might create a gradient background for the sky and use a simple rectangle for that. The ground will be green with darker trees, and a few triangular shapes for the trees will add interest. I’ll provide this in JSON format.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
